data.summary: Software & Systems Engineer combining a background in organizational behavior & development (OBD) with hands-on systems integration. Experienced in low-level applications, enterprise backends, and cloud pipelines, with a track record of validating voice processing software and tuning audio performance directly in vehicles on-site. Uses an understanding of team dynamics and organizational development to build sustainable developer workflows and highly maintainable software systems.

experience.subtitle: Automotive Voice AI | Forward Deployment
highlights: Led the on-site integration, testing, and release of voice processing software for Hyundai’s 2026 vehicle program at their China R&D Center (HMTC).
Conducted on-site vehicle validation and audio testing across international sites in Seoul, Yantai, Sydney, and Dubai.
Partnered with Toyota's engineering team in Sunnyvale to integrate voice software into prototype infotainment systems.
Developed an offline voice-command feature that maps spoken requests directly to vehicle functions without an internet connection.
Configured and prepared demonstration vehicles in Paris for Renault engineering evaluations and client trials.
Managed offline container deployments on edge hardware, ensuring reliable container failover and traffic routing for speech-to-text services.
Built a desktop utility for field engineers to automate audio testing, file management, and system tuning on-site.
Extended the team's internal test library to automate audio recording and channel configuration, reducing script creation time.
Prepared and validated demonstration vehicles for CES 2026.

experience.subtitle: Database Systems & .NET Desktop Tools
highlights: Modernized quality-control reporting systems, upgrading legacy backend services for better performance and maintainability.
Created automated testing and build pipelines in GitLab to streamline development workflows.
Developed and optimized database schemas and SQL queries to manage high-volume production-line quality metrics.
Created desktop administration tools using .NET and WPF to help operators configure industrial monitoring equipment.

experience.subtitle: Data Ingestion & Systems Integration
highlights: Completed Infinity Labs' intensive 32-week systems engineering program (C/C++, memory management, multithreading) before placing on-site.
Customized and maintained high-volume data ingestion pipelines inside secure client intelligence environments.
Troubleshot and resolved database indexing issues and pipeline bottlenecks for high-volume client datasets.
Designed data schemas to import complex records into search indexes for investigative tools.
Wrote robust data transformation scripts to clean and process high-volume information feeds.
Obtained and maintained security clearance for working inside secure client intelligence environments.

projects.name: Personal Website (yanai.sh)
highlights: Built a personal portfolio integrating an Astro frontend with a Cloudflare serverless backend and automated contact form.
Developed a high-speed search feature utilizing a compiled Rust and WebAssembly engine for client-side queries.
Designed automated GitHub Actions pipelines to deploy and proxy serverless release assets automatically.

projects.name: WinWS (Automated Windows ISO Builder)
highlights: Built a WPF-driven PowerShell desktop utility to automate the customization and offline staging of clean Windows 11 installers.
Wrote custom post-install scripts to configure local WSL2 developer environments and set up keyboard-driven window tiling managers.
Completed a Siemens Digital Industries Software case study on post-acquisition integration, interviewing tech leaders to improve cross-team handoffs.
Selected as a graduate teaching assistant for the Mergers & Acquisitions course.
Designed service boundaries and API integration paths for a distributed capstone project.
Studied event-driven, microkernel, and layered architectures, as well as Domain-Driven Design (DDD) concepts.
Built hands-on deployment pipelines utilizing Docker, Kubernetes, Terraform, Ansible, Jenkins, and AWS.
Configured Nginx and Apache web servers as load balancers and reverse proxies.
